title: Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil

seo.title: Rosemary Focaccia Recipe: Authentic Genovese Style
seo.metaDescription: Learn to make the best Rosemary Focaccia recipe, Genovese style! This easy guide uses olive oil for a perfect, airy, and flavorful Italian bread.
longTailKeywords: focaccia genovese rosemary olive oil recipe
seo.imageAlt: Close-up of golden-brown rosemary focaccia genovese with olive oil and sea salt
seo.imageCaption: Bake the perfect Rosemary Focaccia Genovese at home with this easy recipe!

description: Imagine the aroma of freshly baked bread filling your kitchen, a golden crust giving way to an incredibly soft, airy interior. This Rosemary Focaccia Genovese, rich with olive oil and fragrant rosemary, is a true taste of Ligurian sunshine. It is perfect as an appetizer, a side to any meal, or simply enjoyed on its own.

instructions.title: Activate the Yeast
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the warm water and active dry yeast. Stir gently and let it sit for 5-10 minutes until foamy. This step ensures your yeast is active and ready to make your focaccia airy.

instructions.title: Mix the Dough
instructions.text: Add the flour and 10g of fine sea salt to the yeast mixture. Mix with a wooden spoon or your hands until a shaggy dough forms. Gradually incorporate 30ml of the olive oil, mixing until just combined. Do not overmix at this stage.

instructions.title: Knead and First Rise
instructions.text: Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead for 8-10 minutes until smooth and elastic. Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and let it rise in a warm place for 1.5-2 hours, or until doubled in size. This slow rise develops flavor.

instructions.title: Prepare for Baking
instructions.text: Gently deflate the risen dough and transfer it to a 9x13 inch (23x33 cm) baking pan generously coated with the remaining 20ml of olive oil. Using your fingertips, gently press the dough to evenly spread it across the pan. Cover and let it rest for another 30 minutes. This second rest relaxes the gluten.

instructions.title: Dimple and Top
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 220C (425F). Uncover the dough and use your oiled fingertips to create deep dimples across the entire surface. Drizzle generously with more extra virgin olive oil, then sprinkle with chopped fresh rosemary and flaky sea salt. The dimples hold the oil and create pockets of flavor.

instructions.title: Bake to Perfection
instructions.text: Bake for 25-35 minutes, or until the focaccia is golden brown and cooked through. If the top browns too quickly, you can loosely tent it with foil. The high heat creates a beautiful crust.

instructions.title: Cool and Serve
instructions.text: Remove the focaccia from the oven and carefully transfer it to a wire rack to cool slightly. Drizzle with a final touch of olive oil before slicing and serving warm. Enjoy the authentic taste of Genova!

tips: For an extra crispy bottom, preheat your baking pan in the oven for 10 minutes before adding the dough to bake.
Do not be shy with the olive oil! It is crucial for the authentic flavor and texture of Genovese focaccia, especially the generous drizzle before baking.
Focaccia is best enjoyed fresh on the day it is baked. Leftovers can be reheated briefly in a warm oven or toasted for a delightful snack.
article:
Focaccia Genovese, a culinary jewel from the Ligurian coast of Italy, is more than just bread; it's an experience. Imagine a golden-crusted flatbread, generously drizzled with robust olive oil, fragrant with fresh rosemary, and boasting an irresistibly airy, tender crumb. This iconic Italian baking staple has captivated palates worldwide, offering a taste of simple elegance and profound flavor. While many variations exist, the true magic often lies in the Genovese style, characterized by its thinness, its signature dimples, and a liberal hand with high-quality extra virgin olive oil. If you've ever dreamt of recreating that authentic Italian bakery aroma in your own kitchen, diving into a classic Rosemary Focaccia Recipe is the perfect starting point.

## What is Focaccia Genovese?

At its heart, focaccia is an oven-baked Italian flatbread, similar in style and texture to pizza dough. However, Focaccia Genovese distinguishes itself with several key characteristics. Hailing from Genoa, the capital of the Liguria region, it's traditionally known as "fûgassa" in the local dialect. Unlike thicker, more bready focaccias found elsewhere, the Genovese version is typically thinner, resulting in a delightfully crisp exterior and a soft, chewy interior. Its surface is famously dimpled – created by pressing fingers into the dough before baking – which serves to create pockets for the olive oil and coarse sea salt to pool, intensifying the flavor with every bite. The generous use of high-quality extra virgin olive oil is non-negotiable, contributing to its rich flavor, golden hue, and characteristic tenderness. Often, it's infused with fresh rosemary, a combination that has become synonymous with this beloved Italian bread. It's a versatile bread, enjoyed plain, dipped in olive oil, or as a base for simple toppings.

## Ingredients for Rosemary Focaccia

Crafting the perfect focaccia begins with selecting the right ingredients. Quality truly makes a difference here, especially for the olive oil and rosemary. To embark on this delightful Rosemary Focaccia Recipe, you will need:

*   **All-Purpose Flour:** Approximately 500 grams (about 4 cups). While specialty bread flour can be used for a chewier texture, good quality all-purpose flour works wonderfully.
*   **Warm Water:** Around 350-400 ml (1.5 - 1.75 cups), ideally between 105-115°F (40-46°C). The exact amount may vary based on your flour and humidity.
*   **Active Dry Yeast:** 7 grams (2 ¼ teaspoons or one packet).
*   **Fine Sea Salt:** 10 grams (1.5 teaspoons) for the dough, plus extra coarse sea salt for sprinkling on top.
*   **Extra Virgin Olive Oil:** At least 60 ml (¼ cup) for the dough, plus a generous amount (another ¼ cup or more) for drizzling, coating the pan, and finishing. Choose a good quality, fruity olive oil.
*   **Fresh Rosemary:** 2-3 sprigs, leaves stripped and roughly chopped. Fresh is paramount for that authentic aroma and flavor.

These simple ingredients, when combined with care and patience, transform into an extraordinary loaf.

## Step-by-Step Rosemary Focaccia Instructions

Following this Rosemary Focaccia Recipe is a rewarding journey, resulting in a bread that will impress and delight.

1.  **Activate the Yeast:** In a large bowl, combine the warm water and yeast. Let it sit for 5-10 minutes until foamy, indicating the yeast is active.
2.  **Mix the Dough:** Add the flour and fine sea salt to the yeast mixture. Stir with a wooden spoon or your hands until a shaggy dough forms. Drizzle in 2 tablespoons of olive oil.
3.  **Knead (or No-Knead):** For a traditional approach, turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead for 8-10 minutes until smooth and elastic. Alternatively, for a no-knead method, simply cover the bowl and let it rest for 15-20 minutes, then perform a series of "stretch and folds" every 20-30 minutes for about 1.5-2 hours. This develops the gluten without intensive kneading.
4.  **First Rise (Bulk Fermentation):** Lightly oil a clean bowl. Place the dough in the bowl, turn it to coat with oil, cover with plastic wrap or a damp towel, and let it rise in a warm place for 1.5-2 hours, or until doubled in size.
5.  **Prepare the Pan:** Generously drizzle a 9x13 inch baking pan (or a similar size) with olive oil, ensuring the bottom and sides are well coated.
6.  **Shape and Dimple:** Gently transfer the risen dough to the prepared pan. Using your fingertips, gently stretch and press the dough to fill the pan. Don't force it; if it resists, let it rest for 10 minutes and try again. Once spread, generously drizzle more olive oil over the surface. Use your fingertips to create deep dimples all over the dough, pressing almost to the bottom of the pan. Sprinkle with chopped fresh rosemary and coarse sea salt.
7.  **Second Rise (Proofing):** Cover the pan loosely with plastic wrap or a clean kitchen towel and let it rise again in a warm place for 30-45 minutes, or until visibly puffy.
8.  **Preheat Oven:** While the dough is proofing, preheat your oven to 425°F (220°C).
9.  **Bake:** Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the focaccia is golden brown on top and cooked through. The bottom should also be nicely browned and crisp.
10. **Finish:** As soon as it comes out of the oven, drizzle a little more extra virgin olive oil over the hot focaccia. This enhances the flavor and keeps it moist. Let it cool slightly in the pan before transferring to a wire rack to cool completely, or serve warm.

## Tips for Perfect Focaccia Bread

Mastering the Rosemary Focaccia Recipe involves a few key insights that can elevate your bake from good to outstanding:

*   **Quality Olive Oil is Key:** This cannot be stressed enough. Focaccia is an olive oil vehicle. Invest in a good quality extra virgin olive oil; its flavor will shine through.
*   **Don't Rush the Rise:** Patience is a virtue in bread making. Allow the dough ample time to rise in both stages. A slow, cool rise (even overnight in the fridge for the first rise) can develop deeper flavors.
*   **Hydration Matters:** Focaccia dough is typically quite wet and sticky. Don't be tempted to add too much extra flour during mixing or kneading, as this will result in a dry, dense focaccia. Embrace the stickiness!
*   **Embrace the Dimples:** Those signature dimples aren't just for show. They create little wells for the olive oil and salt, concentrating flavor and contributing to the focaccia's unique texture. Don't be shy about pressing firmly.
*   **Generous Seasoning:** Don't skimp on the coarse sea salt and fresh rosemary. They are essential components of the Genovese experience.
*   **Hot Oven, Hot Pan:** Baking focaccia in a hot oven ensures a crisp crust and a quick rise. Some bakers even preheat their empty baking pan in the oven for 10-15 minutes before adding the dough, for an extra crispy bottom.

## Serving Suggestions for Focaccia

Once you've perfected your Rosemary Focaccia Recipe, the ways to enjoy it are endless.

*   **Simply Plain:** The pure flavor of fresh focaccia, especially when warm, is often best enjoyed on its own or with a simple dip in high-quality olive oil and balsamic vinegar.
*   **Appetizer or Antipasto:** Serve alongside a platter of cured meats (prosciutto, salami), cheeses (Parmigiano-Reggiano, fresh mozzarella), and marinated olives.
*   **Soup or Salad Companion:** Its hearty yet airy texture makes it an excellent accompaniment to a robust minestrone, a light tomato soup, or a fresh garden salad.
*   **Sandwiches:** Slice focaccia horizontally to make incredible sandwiches. Fill with grilled vegetables, pesto, mozzarella, or classic Italian deli meats.
*   **Breakfast or Brunch:** A slice of focaccia can be a delightful addition to a brunch spread, perhaps alongside scrambled eggs or a frittata.
*   **With Wine:** A glass of crisp white wine or a light red pairs beautifully with the savory notes of rosemary and olive oil.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Rosemary Focaccia Recipe is a masterpiece, it also serves as a fantastic canvas for culinary creativity.

*   **Other Herbs:** Experiment with different fresh herbs like thyme, oregano, marjoram, or a blend of Italian herbs.
*   **Savory Toppings:** Before the second rise or just before baking, add toppings such as sliced cherry tomatoes, Kalamata olives, thinly sliced red onion, caramelized onions, sun-dried tomatoes, or even a sprinkle of grated Parmesan cheese.
*   **Sweet Focaccia:** While less traditional for Genovese, focaccia can be made sweet. Try sprinkling sugar, adding thinly sliced apples or grapes, or a drizzle of honey after baking.
*   **Flour Blends:** Incorporate a percentage of whole wheat flour for a nuttier flavor and denser crumb, or a touch of semolina flour for extra crispness.
*   **Garlic Infusion:** Mince some garlic and add it along with the rosemary, or infuse your olive oil with garlic cloves before drizzling.
*   **Gluten-Free:** While challenging to replicate the exact texture, many gluten-free flour blends are available that can be adapted for focaccia, often requiring more hydration and careful handling.

Armed with this comprehensive Rosemary Focaccia Recipe, you're ready to transform simple ingredients into an extraordinary taste of Italy. Whether you stick to the pure Genovese form or explore delightful variations, the joy of baking and sharing this aromatic bread is an experience to savor. Buon appetito!
